title: HAVw A5 Juggernaut
content:
{{Top|leg|canon=Juggernaut A5}} {{Otheruses|Juggernaut}} {{GroundVehicle |image=[[File:Turbotank_negvv.png]] |name=HAVw A5 Juggernaut |designer= |manufacturer=[[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' |line= |model= |type=[[Heavy assault vehicle]][[Imperial Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''Imperial Sourcebook'', Second Edition]] |cost=*200,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] {{C|new}}''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' *120,000 credits {{C|used}} |modifier= |sysmods= |length=21.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |width= |height=10 meters {{C|main body}}, rising to nearly 15 meters including sentry tower |mass= |max accel= |speed=200 [[Kilometer/Legends|km]][[Standard hour/Legends|h]], reduced to 25 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kmh]] while turning |velocity= |engine= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=*3 [[Mk 1e/W heavy laser cannon]]s''[[Star Wars Miniatures Battles Companion]]'' *1 [[Mk 3/W medium blaster cannon]] or 1 double medium [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] *2 [[Mk 2/W concussion grenade launcher]]s each with six [[Mk 4/W concussion grenade]]s |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=*2 [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]]''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' *6 [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] *1 sentry |skeleton= |passengers=50 ([[Soldier/Legends|troops]]) |capacity=1 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric ton]] |cargohandling= |consumables=1 [[Standard week/Legends|week]]''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]'' |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |availability=*Common *Military *Licensed |role= |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] *[[United Warlord Fleets]][[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']] *[[Imperial Remnant]] }} {{Quote|Day 17: Forward drive shaft on Juggernaut-11 has snapped. Requesting replacement drive shaft.''"
"''Day 22: Resubmitting request for replacement drive shaft.''"
"''Day 38: Drive shaft still has not arrived. Please advise.''"
"''Day 50: Awaiting drive shaft.''"
"''Day 123: Awaiting drive shaft.|Excerpt from log of Imperial machinist stationed on [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]]|''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''}} The '''HAVw A5 Juggernaut''' was a massive, ten-wheeled combat vehicle produced by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]. It was used by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. ==Description== Designed and built by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]], the HAVw A5 Juggernaut was a 21.8 [[Meter/Legends|meter]] long wheeled assault vehicle. The main body of the A5 stood ten meters tall, with the separate sentry tower increasing the overall height of the Juggernaut to nearly fifteen meters. Equipped with thick, nearly impenetrable armor, the A5 incorporated dual control cabins, one located at either end of the vehicle. As a result, the A5 was steerable from either end, and the wheels were independently steerable from either control cabin. The dual steering control system was required due to the amount of time required to turn the vehicle completely around; in situations requiring the vehicle to reverse course, it could simply be driven from the other end of the vehicle. [[File:Juggernaut_egvv.jpg|thumb|left|A5 Juggernaut schematics]] The A5 Juggernaut was driven by five sets of massive wheels, with large [[bump flexor]]s providing shock support along with a flexible system of shocks and servos. However, the wheels lacked traction, and the use of independent drive units meant that the A5 lacked the maneuverability or the momentum to overcome certain rough or uneven terrains. The drive system was also awkward to operate, requiring precise coordination between the two pilots to keep the wheels in line during maneuvers to prevent locking the wheels up and immobilizing the vehicle. Juggernauts with damaged drive systems awaiting replacement parts could spend weeks waiting for repairs. A5 Juggernauts could reach a respectable speed of two hundred [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] in a straight line, but was slow to turn, dropping to a paltry twenty-five kilometers an hour. Mounted atop the Juggernaut was a sentry tower incorporating a [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] array that required additional height to function properly, as well as a communications antenna. The information gathered by the sensor array was relayed to the [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]] positions to aid in targeting. Without information from the tower, the A5 was forced to rely on information attained from ground level sensors, which in turn meant that the A5 was vulnerable to attacks from aircraft or vehicles out of direct line-of-sight. Its profile meant it was the most vulnerable and exposed point on the A5, and it was the least wanted posting for an A5 crew member. The A5 mounted multiple weapons systems including three [[Mk 1e/W heavy laser cannon]]s one of which was turret mounted on the roof of the forward command cabin, and one each on either side of the vehicle covering the port and starboard fire arcs mounted on the rear quarter of the A5. The range of the laser cannons were two-thirds that of comparable weapons on a walker. A turret-mounted [[Mk 3/W medium blaster cannon]] or double medium blaster cannon was situated on the roof of the A5 on the centerline. Its range and stopping power were better than a walker's chin-mounted weapons. Two [[Mk 2/W concussion grenade launcher]]s were equipped on the same turret as the medium blaster cannon, located under retractable covers. Each launcher had a magazine of either ten [[Concussion grenade/Legends|concussion grenades]] or six [[Mk 4/W concussion grenade]]s. The firepower mounted on the A5 was comparable to that of an AT-AT [[Walker/Legends|walker]]. The A5 required two [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], one in either command cabin, and six [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] to operate the various weapon systems. Another trooper was required for the sentry tower to provide target information for the gunnery positions. The A5 was primarily a troop transport, and could carry fifty [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] in [[Power armor/Legends|power armor]] along with all necessary equipment. It could also be reconfigured to carry light assault vehicles such as six [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]], two light combat assault [[Landspeeder/Legends|speeders]], or other small [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]]. The A5's ability to carry troops and small assault vehicles matched or in some cases exceeded that of a [[Walker/Legends|walker]]. [[File:A5_Juggernaut_TFUCG.jpg|thumb|right|An A6 Juggernaut, the larger successor of the A5]] A5 Juggernauts cost 200,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to purchase new, and 120,000 used. Each vehicle had a cargo storage capacity of one [[Metric ton/Legends|metric ton]], and a [[Standard week/Legends|week's]] supply of consumables. Design elements for the A5 Juggernaut were incorporated into the later [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transport]]. ==History== The A5 Juggernaut was a predecessor to the AT-AT. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] passed a resolution allowing [[Planet/Legends|planets]] in demilitarized systems to create their own local defense forces to assist in repelling [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] incursions. With Republic financial support and backing, Kuat Drive Yards was contracted to supply assault vehicles to the new local planetary defense forces, resulting in the creation of the A5 Juggernaut. Sharing design elements with the [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer/Legends|All Terrain Tactical Enforcer]], the A5 was intended to be a larger, wheeled version of the AT-TE, with identical armor plating and a similar command cabin. Referred to as the "rolling slab" by its crew, the A5 inspired fear in those who opposed it. Playing a prominent role in military forces throughout the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], the A5 was considered to be a state of the art combat vehicle when it was introduced. The A5 was sold to [[Governor/Legends|governors]], corporations, and private interests across the Outer Rim, and it also fell into the hands of criminal enterprises, including ones related to the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]]. The unique drive system of the A5 made them effective combatants over rough terrain and urban environments. [[File:Juggernautyavin.jpg|thumb|left|An A5 Juggernaut escorted by stormtroopers]] The A5 Juggernaut was one of the most powerful ground vehicles deployed during the Clone Wars, and were versatile combatants, deployed to spearhead ground assaults or engage in hit and run operations. They could also be employed as mobile command centers, or as anti-aircraft artillery against [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] and low-flying [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. By [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the A5 had been supplanted in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] by the considerably larger [[HAVw A6 Juggernaut/Legends|A6 model]].{{Film|III}} With the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], many A5s serving in planetary defense forces were commandeered by the Empire for use in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. They were generally deployed when AT-AT walkers were either not available, or terrain considerations prevented their use. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Empire owned and maintained more A5 Juggernauts than any other concern. As newer and more modern combat vehicles were introduced, the Empire began retiring their stock of A5s, or abandoned their wrecks on the battlefield. The Empire considered the resources required to recover the hulks to not be worth the money or effort, allowing criminal enterprises, private companies, and insurgent forces to attain them. By the time of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], few A5 Juggernauts remained operational. Those that still saw service required skilled mechanics and constant maintenance to keep them combat-ready. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of HAVw A5 Juggernauts}} *{{TCWComicCite|6.50|The
